articles.title: IMD issues yellow warning for heat wave and thunderstorm activities for Odisha - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a series of weather alerts for Odisha, including a heatwave and thunderstorm warnings, effective through the coming days. The state is expected to experience extremely hot and humid conditions, with high temperatures reaching dangerous levels in certain districts.
As of today, April 15, the weather department has warned that hot and humid conditions will prevail over the districts of Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati. These conditions are expected to persist until at least April 18, affecting daily life and increasing the risk of heat-related illnesses.
The forecast for April 16 to 17 predicts the occurrence of heatwave conditions in Bolangir, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, and Sonepur, while thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds (up to 30-40 kmph) will likely impact areas like Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, and Balasore. Residents of these districts are advised to remain cautious, especially during the afternoon and evening hours.
By April 18, the IMD has predicted light to moderate rain or thunderstorms at one or two locations in the southern and central districts, including Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, and Malkangiri. These storms are expected to bring relief from the oppressive heat, though the risk of thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds will continue.
In addition, the heatwave conditions are expected to stretch to districts like Sundergarh, Bargarh, and Angul during the upcoming days, with specific heat warnings extending through April 21. The IMD has emphasized the need for the public to stay hydrated, avoid outdoor activities during peak heat hours, and take necessary precautions against the scorching weather.

Authorities have also urged people in affected regions to stay alert for any updates and adhere to safety guidelines as thunderstorms and gusty winds could lead to temporary disruptions. The weather patterns are expected to shift after April 21, with a gradual decrease in the intensity of the heatwaves and thunderstorms.
